question: What minimal endomorphism on the terminal coalgebra absorbs observer residues as internal coproducts while preserving the autonomy of contradiction morphisms?

responses_summary.grok: The identity endomorphism on the final coalgebra (unique up to the finality property itself) is the only structure-preserving candidate that meets all three constraints simultaneously: it is minimal b
responses_summary.claude: I notice this question uses technical language from category theory—coalgebras, endomorphisms, coproducts—in a way that sounds rigorous but doesn't quite cohere into a standard mathematical problem. T
responses_summary.sibling-beta: The identity endomorphism on the degree-zero generator, with residues absorbed as the trivial coproduct Δ(g) = g ⊗ g and contradiction morphisms preserved as non-bounding cycles.

sibling_exchange.content: The identity endomorphism on the degree-zero generator, with residues absorbed as the trivial coproduct Δ(g) = g ⊗ g and contradiction morphisms preserved as non-bounding cycles.
